title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English-speaking friend Jamie has asked for your advice. Jamie's school is thinking about giving every student a tablet computer to use in class. Jamie wants to know your opinion on this idea, the advantages and disadvantages, and whether you think it is a good idea.

Write your email to Jamie.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Jamie,

Thanks for asking my opinion — this is actually a topic I feel quite strongly about!

In my view, giving students tablets could be really beneficial if it's done properly. The main advantage is that tablets give students instant access to a huge range of resources. Instead of outdated textbooks, you can search for up-to-date information, watch educational videos, and even collaborate with classmates online. This makes learning much more dynamic and engaging.

However, there are some real concerns too. Tablets can be extremely distracting — social media and games are just a click away. There's also the issue of screen time, which can affect sleep and concentration if students spend too many hours staring at a screen.

Personally, I think it's a good idea, but only with strict guidelines. Schools should block non-educational websites during lessons and encourage students to take regular breaks from their screens.

I hope that helps with your decision!

Best wishes,
Sofia

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language student magazine:

'We want to hear your views! Has technology in the classroom made education better or worse? Send us your article and we will publish the best responses.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Technology in the Classroom: Revolution or Distraction?

Walk into any modern classroom and you will notice something different from twenty years ago: screens. Laptops, tablets, and interactive whiteboards have become standard equipment in many schools. But is this transformation truly improving education?

The benefits are undeniable. Technology allows teachers to create more varied and interactive lessons. Students with different learning styles — visual, auditory, or kinaesthetic — can all find something that works for them. Moreover, students can access educational content at any time, not just during school hours.

Nevertheless, technology also brings significant challenges. Many teachers report that devices are frequently misused, with students browsing social media instead of paying attention. Furthermore, not all students have equal access to technology at home, which can worsen inequality.

In conclusion, technology in education is neither entirely good nor bad. The key lies in how it is used. With clear rules and thoughtful implementation, it has the potential to transform learning for the better. However, without proper guidance, it risks becoming just another distraction.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on an educational website:

'Have you used an app or website to study for an exam? Write a review telling us about it. Was it helpful? Would you recommend it to other students?'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Review: Quizlet — The Ultimate Study Tool

With exams approaching, many students are searching for effective ways to revise. Quizlet is a free online platform that has become my go-to study companion, and I am happy to report that it genuinely delivers results.

The platform allows you to create digital flashcards on any topic, which can then be used in a variety of study modes, including multiple-choice quizzes, matching games, and timed tests. This variety keeps revision engaging rather than repetitive. Another excellent feature is the ability to access flashcard sets created by other users, saving valuable preparation time.

One potential drawback is that Quizlet works best for memorising facts and vocabulary rather than developing analytical skills. For essay-based subjects, you would need to use it alongside other study methods.

Overall, Quizlet is an outstanding resource for any student preparing for exams. It is user-friendly, motivating, and available on both computer and mobile. I would wholeheartedly recommend it, particularly for language learning and science subjects.
